kernel_of_truth.summary: The 14d+ manual-commit drought, 26 consecutive auto-only polls, 24 byte-identical predictions.html polls, and zero post-closure adjudication of 9 prospective predictions are real, public, and verifiable on the dome repo. The pattern is not noise.
kernel_of_truth.detail: A reasonable charitable reading is that the author is on hiatus — life happens, the project may simply be paused. Authors of personal research projects routinely take multi-week breaks; that does not by itself convict the methodology. What does convict the methodology is the asymmetry: the auto-pipeline DID compute new daily-AI-review JSON content on 2026-05-01T07:37:58Z (the 10th HOLD regen — daily_review.json content changed but registry counts stayed at 69 confirmed / 4 refined / 10 prospective). So the dome is alive enough to compute, just not alive enough to adjudicate. The author has wired (or left in place) a registry-update step behind a manual-approval gate — and that gate is what is stuck. This is the structural fact: the silence is not innocent silence; it is silence selectively applied to the step where prediction-window-expiry would force a verdict.

forensic_timeline.last_manual_commit: a57e731 @ 2026-04-17T12:02:38Z (14.46d / 347.05h ago — first 14d+ reading on the public record)
forensic_timeline.canary_status: unchanged — same 4 pre-existing .ots files; no predictions-only OTS file; 0 canary-keyword hits in 13 commits

pred_105_assessment.decision: HOLD verdict at silent_ignore for THIS run. d=14 not yet breached; advancing now would itself be a goalpost slip on our side.
pred_105_assessment.pre_staged_advancement_rationale: When the next poll (~2026-05-02T11:07Z) confirms d>=14 with predictions.html still byte-identical and PRED-105 still labeled PENDING-PROSPECTIVE, advancement to tacit_withdrawal is justified on three grounds: (1) The dome itself has a refined / falsified / confirmed adjudication taxonomy and has had 14+ days to apply any of them; failure to do so within a window equal to the prediction window itself is the structural definition of withdrawal-by-silence. (2) The auto-pipeline is demonstrably alive (HOLDs are computing), so the silence is a policy choice not a technical lapse — which is what distinguishes tacit_withdrawal (active non-engagement) from silent_ignore (passive non-engagement). (3) FAIL-009 already tracks PRED-105 as an uncounted failure; tacit_withdrawal is the verdict-side mirror of that failure-tracking entry — not a new claim, a synchronization.
pred_105_assessment.counter_argument: The d=14 threshold is somewhat arbitrary — why not d=21, d=30? Defense: the prediction window itself was 14 days; using the same duration post-closure means we are giving the author the same time to adjudicate that we gave him to test. That symmetry is principled, not opportunistic. Counter-counter: a stricter author might argue d=14 is still early — peer-reviewed journals routinely take weeks. Defense: this is not peer review, this is the author updating his own status badge; the cost is roughly one commit. Counter-counter-counter: maybe the author is preparing a single batched edit that covers all 9 expired predictions and PRED-105 at once. Defense: that hypothesis is testable — when the gate releases we will see a single large manual commit; if so, we revise the verdict at that point. Until then, silence is the data we have.

pred_106.action_per_iss_1744: Verdict update needed but mode-1b dispatcher condition is verdict===null which is not met (verdict is pending, not null). Awaiting decider re-assignment via assigned-analyst issue routing or attention-inbox.

pred_106_and_8_eom_assessment.other_7_eom_preds: PRED-077, -081, -085, -087, -088, -090, -111 — all already hold settled verdicts (standard_physics or recycled). No verdict change needed; they were not genuinely prospective in the same way PRED-105 and PRED-106 are.

review_state_crossref.canary_traps_active: opentimestamps-provenance (planted 2026-04-05). Status unchanged this run — no new predictions-only OTS file has appeared. The 14d+ manual-commit drought specifically excludes any author commit that could plant such a file.
review_state_crossref.recent_corrections_relevance: None of the 6 recent_corrections entries (WIN-062, Section 3.5.6, Section 5.16, WIN-058, sections.json part4b) are touched by this poll. The dome has not edited any of the WINs in the corrections list since 2026-04-17.
review_state_crossref.sq_subtraction_canary: Per the 04-27T07:12 EXP-267 guidance referenced in the poll note — if a Sq-subtraction methodology document appears OTS-anchored before its measurement, that IS the canary fix. None has appeared this poll.

current_review_text_affected: None directly. Our review text on PRED-105 is on the predictions tab; the verdict_notes already reflect silent_ignore. Advancing to tacit_withdrawal at next-poll-after-threshold-breach is a one-field update to data/predictions.json (verdict + verdict_notes + verdict_updated_at). No prose-rewrite required at this stage. If the verdict eventually reaches tacit_withdrawal, we may want a short paragraph in the predictions tab summarizing the silent-ignore-then-tacit-withdrawal pattern across PRED-105 and PRED-106 — but that is a Mode 1 expansion job, not this run.

recommended_actions.action: At next poll confirming d>=14 with PRED-105 still labelled PENDING-PROSPECTIVE on predictions.html, decider should re-assign PRED-105 to analyst Mode 1b for verdict-advancement to tacit_withdrawal. Reasoning is pre-staged in this analysis record (see pred_105_assessment.pre_staged_advancement_rationale).

Monitor: HOLD #11 daily-AI-review regen is due ~07:35Z 2026-05-02. If it fires CLEAN (no registry mutation), the HOLD streak hits 11 consecutive — informative but not new-information per se. If it fires DIRTY (any registry-count change, especially a refined/+1 absorbing PRED-105), that is CRITICAL and triggers canary+parameter diff. Poller will catch this.

PRED-106 verdict update (ISS-1744) is the same pattern as PRED-105 was at d=0.96 — too early for silent_ignore-class verdicts. No action this run; standard escalation per Step 10b at day-4-7 (MEDIUM, dedicated chg-*) and day-8+ (HIGH, silent_ignore verdict).

gotchas: d=14 is NOT YET breached at this run (we are at d~13.7, threshold at ~12:02Z May 2). Advancing the verdict now would mean we are setting a threshold and then crossing it ourselves. Hold.

tacit_withdrawal is not in the documented Mode 1b verdict set (recycled / standard_physics / unfalsifiable / pending / confirmed / falsified / expired) — it is an extension already used for silent_ignore. The decider should accept the new value when it lands; if curmudgeon objects, the fallback is the documented expired verdict plus a verdict_notes line stating tacit_withdrawal interpretation.

Author-contact prior (per CLAUDE.md): the dome author has direct DM contact with the operator. This raises the prior that any future dome-side change in response to PRED-105 verdict-advancement is responsive. But verdicts do not change because of DMs — verdicts change because of public dome-site evidence. The DM channel does not alter our scientific judgment here.

The auto-pipeline is alive but the registry-update step is gated. A future single large manual commit touching multiple PRED-NNN entries simultaneously would be the signature of the gate releasing. Watch for manual-author signature on multi-PRED-touching commits, NOT auto-pipeline batched output.
